jokes.text: So, Will, I'm off like a dirty shirt.
jokes.setup: Guest musician finishing up at the Banks house
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned slang expression delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from dated slang that feels authentic to the character

jokes.text: There's definitely some perks to working for NBC.
jokes.setup: Will commenting on having a musician friend
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to acknowledge the show airs on NBC
jokes.explanation: Classic Fresh Prince fourth-wall break, perfectly delivered by Will Smith's character

jokes.text: You can sleep under the couch, you squidgit.
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton arguing over who gets the couch
jokes.punchline: Made-up insult 'squidgit' combining 'squid' and 'midget'
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that's both insulting Carlton's height and creating a memorable portmanteau

jokes.text: These are big, mean, nasty, bug-eyed...take-over-the-human-race kind of crickets, man.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil downplaying the cricket problem
jokes.punchline: Will's dramatic overstatement of cricket threat level
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from harmless to apocalyptic, fits Will's tendency to overdramatize

jokes.text: Listen, if you're gonna hold the popcorn, make sure you hold it up in plain sight...so when he go reaching for the bucket...he don't miss, talking about, 'Oops.'
jokes.setup: Will giving Ashley dating advice
jokes.punchline: Exposing the classic 'accidental' groping move
jokes.explanation: Relatable dating wisdom delivered with Will's street-smart protective brother energy

jokes.text: And if he tells you to pull his finger, don't do it.
jokes.setup: Will continuing his protective dating advice
jokes.punchline: Classic juvenile prank warning
jokes.explanation: Standard bathroom humor but fits the protective brother context

jokes.text: Daddy never gave Hilary a curfew. - He was probably hoping she wouldn't come back.
jokes.setup: Ashley complaining about different treatment
jokes.punchline: Will's savage burn about Hilary being unwanted
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline structure with a brutal but character-appropriate roast

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, he probably thought you was hot, creamy and rich. - Oh, yeah, that's it. I was pretty sure I wasn't mountain-grown.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying director said she reminded him of coffee
jokes.punchline: Will's sexual innuendo followed by Hilary's innocent coffee reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent double-layer joke where Will makes it sexual and Hilary completely misses it, staying in coffee metaphor

jokes.text: You know what? I know how we can make it even more like the study center. - I won't be here.
jokes.setup: Carlton excited about quiet study time together
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt rejection
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on Carlton's earnestness vs Will's avoidance

jokes.text: I really don't feel like hearing about you and your little friends, okay?
jokes.setup: Carlton starting to tell the grasshopper and ant fable
jokes.punchline: Will interpreting it as Carlton talking about actual friends
jokes.explanation: Solid misunderstanding joke that also roasts Carlton's social life

jokes.text: Yup, even if we were insects, I'd be bigger than you.
jokes.setup: Carlton finishing the grasshopper and ant moral
jokes.punchline: Will turning the moral into a height joke
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the moral lesson that's perfectly in character for Will to make it about Carlton's height

jokes.text: I cracked it earlier when I had to write down...this honey's phone number right next to the periodical table.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Will is just now opening his book
jokes.punchline: Will's double meaning of 'cracked' and misnamed periodic table
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Will's priorities (girls over studying) plus malapropism with 'periodical table'

jokes.text: Little Timmy took a drink But he will drink no more For what he thought was H2O Was H2SO4
jokes.setup: Carlton offering to share a chemistry rhyme
jokes.punchline: Dark rhyme about accidental poisoning
jokes.explanation: Classic dark academic humor with good rhyme structure, though slightly out of character for Carlton

jokes.text: Little Carlton was a boy Whose body was short and stumpy He never shut his mouth So I kicked his little rumpy
jokes.setup: Carlton's chemistry rhyme
jokes.punchline: Will's improvised insulting response rhyme
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure using Carlton's rhyme format to roast him about height and talkativeness

jokes.text: Little Carlton was a boy Whose body was short and stumpy / He never shut his mouth So I kicked his little rumpy
jokes.setup: Carlton shares an educational chemistry poem
jokes.punchline: Will parodies it with a crude poem about kicking Carlton
jokes.explanation: Predictable response from Will but fits his character perfectly - crude parody of Carlton's earnestness

jokes.text: I haven't had one this good since I was strip searched at Dulles Airport.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey complaining about being woken from a good dream
jokes.punchline: Unexpected sexual reference to airport security
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark humor from the butler character, subverting his usual propriety

jokes.text: Well, these fingers can work magic. Ain't that what the honeys tell me, huh?
jokes.setup: Will successfully getting baby to stop crying
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about manual dexterity
jokes.explanation: Typical Will swagger but predictable sexual reference

jokes.text: I've never seen a mugger at the Pussycat Theater.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil worrying about movie theater dangers
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing knowledge of adult theater
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing Uncle Phil's unexpected familiarity with adult venues

jokes.text: Idiot.
jokes.setup: Philip realizes he was wrong about Ashley
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's single-word dismissive comment under his breath
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey reaction - maximum impact with minimum words, perfect character beat

jokes.text: Maybe we should just try a bottle and a warm bath. - Thought you'd never ask.
jokes.setup: Vivian suggesting baby care routine
jokes.punchline: Will pretending she's talking to him
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection joke but well-executed timing

jokes.text: It's not made from nuts, it's from beans, so I guess I'm beans about coffee.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying she's 'nuts about coffee'
jokes.punchline: Her literal-minded correction creating nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - overthinking a simple expression in a completely illogical way

jokes.text: I've always wondered, if coffee's made from beans...does that make it a vegetable?
jokes.setup: Hilary continuing her coffee thoughts
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical classification question
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hilary logic - seems reasonable on surface but completely wrong, perfectly in character

jokes.text: What do you think is propping up my makeup table?
jokes.setup: Will asking if she has a dictionary
jokes.punchline: Revealing she uses dictionary as furniture support
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual reveal of Hilary's priorities - books as furniture, not reading material

jokes.text: Yeah, you also thought Tupac Shakur was a Jewish holiday.
jokes.setup: Ashley pointing out Carlton was wrong about something
jokes.punchline: Will's reference to Carlton's cultural ignorance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural reference that perfectly captures Carlton's sheltered upbringing vs Will's street knowledge

jokes.text: Look, Ashley, listen. Guys are only after two things. - I thought they were only after one. - Well, yeah, but they wanna do it more than once.
jokes.setup: Will giving Ashley relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Ashley's innocent correction and Will's clarification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with Ashley being more knowing than expected, then Will's practical clarification

jokes.text: Amazon women had just bound me with sinuous vines...and were taunting me with tangelos.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey describing his interrupted dream
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and sexual fantasy with random fruit
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird sexual fantasy that's perfectly in character for Geoffrey's dry wit

jokes.text: What the hell is a tangelo? - 'Tangelo', the fruit of a hybrid citrus tree...that is a cross between certain varieties of grapefruit and tangerine.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's confusion about Geoffrey's dream fruit
jokes.punchline: Hilary providing precise dictionary definition
jokes.explanation: Good callback to dictionary reading setup, with Hilary's overly precise definition contrasting Uncle Phil's confusion

jokes.text: Yes, perhaps the scent of sea breeze...will recall some painful memory for him, and then he'll commit suicide.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil suggesting air freshener might work on cricket
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's elaborate theory about cricket psychology
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and absurd reasoning from Geoffrey, treating pest control like psychology

jokes.text: Remember last year when I skipped school...so I could pre-register for jury duty?
jokes.setup: Carlton blackmailing Ashley with past snitching
jokes.punchline: Revealing his absurdly civic-minded reason for skipping school
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - skipping school for the most responsible reason possible. Brilliant subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: Must be the ventriloquist of the animal kingdom.
jokes.setup: Cricket sound seeming to come from multiple places
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's elaborate metaphor for cricket sound
jokes.explanation: Decent metaphor but not particularly sharp or character-specific

jokes.text: Now I know how Captain Ahab felt when he was chasing Moby Dick. - Between the two of you, the cricket is the whale?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil comparing himself to Captain Ahab
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's fat joke disguised as literary analysis
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup-punchline with Geoffrey cleverly insulting Uncle Phil's weight through literary comparison

jokes.text: 'Unemployed. Out of work, jobless, not being used. I.E., a person who does not have a job.' See, the answer is right at your fingertips.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil threatening Geoffrey's job
jokes.punchline: Hilary helpfully defining unemployment from dictionary
jokes.explanation: Good callback to dictionary theme, showing Hilary's complete social obliviousness

jokes.text: Oh, well. I guess sometimes that little hardworking head...just gets squashed into a little black spot, huh?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil accidentally destroying Carlton's paper while killing cricket
jokes.punchline: Will connecting the grasshopper/ant moral to Carlton's literal fate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that turns Carlton's moral lesson into dark irony - the hardworking ant gets crushed anyway

jokes.text: Yeah, I was only doing it to get in good with the professor. Oh, well, I guess I'll just have to bring him some cupcakes.
jokes.setup: Carlton revealing his paper was optional
jokes.punchline: His naive solution to academic networking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's innocent approach to academic politics

jokes.text: Not exactly. The second definition of throttle is, 'To strangle, choke.' Killing is the result of throttling.
jokes.setup: Will asking if throttling is like killing
jokes.punchline: Hilary's precise dictionary definition of violent terms
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Hilary's mechanical definition delivery, making violence sound academic

jokes.text: Or whenever you move out of the house. Whatever comes first.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil grounding Ashley for 10 years
jokes.punchline: Adding the whichever-comes-first clause
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the punishment with practical parental logic

jokes.text: Ding-dong, the cricket's dead Ashley's grounded Now you all go to bed
jokes.setup: Multiple resolved plot threads
jokes.punchline: Will's improvised song summarizing the night
jokes.explanation: Decent wrap-up song that efficiently summarizes multiple plot resolutions

jokes.text: The Menendez brothers will be free before I am. - The Menendez brothers got home on time.
jokes.setup: Ashley complaining about her long punishment
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's dark comparison to actual criminals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor connecting curfew violations to famous murder case with perfect parental logic

jokes.text: Well, the only chemistry I needed...was between me and the honey in the next seat. You know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Carlton asking how Will passed without studying
jokes.punchline: Will's double meaning of 'chemistry' implying cheating
jokes.explanation: Standard wordplay with predictable cheating implication, fits character but not inspired

jokes.text: You're not really gonna let people think you cheated?
jokes.setup: Will implying he cheated
jokes.punchline: Carlton's moral concern about reputation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response showing his ethical concerns, but predictable
